description: Identifies aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm resources using high-resolution (1-second) period when standard resolution (60-second) is sufficient, generating avoidable custom metric cost.

cost.pricing_source: Static fallback estimate. Prefer AWS Pricing MCP, AWS Pricing API, or cost_report pricing_note when available.
cost.api_overhead_note: High-resolution metrics require more frequent PutMetricData API calls (up to 60x more than standard). Add API cost savings from cost report when available.

use_cases: Payment fraud detection requiring sub-minute response
Real-time safety or emergency shutdown systems
SLA breach alerts with sub-60-second contractual requirements
Application health alarms evaluated over 5+ minutes
General operational alerting with no sub-minute SLA

scenario_attribute_mapping.note: Scenario files may use non-standard Terraform attributes to encode resolution intent. Map to real provider attributes for optimized Terraform output.

mapping.evaluation_period_minutes>=1: evaluation window >= 60 seconds; sub-minute data granularity provides no benefit

severity_rules.name: high_resolution_with_sufficient_standard
severity_rules.description: Alarm is configured for 1-second high-resolution but scenario evidence indicates 60-second standard resolution is operationally sufficient. Downgrade period to 60 seconds to reduce custom metric cost from $0.90 to $0.30/metric/month.

severity_rules.name: high_resolution_with_long_evaluation_period
severity_rules.description: Alarm evaluates over 1+ minute windows. CloudWatch alarms aggregate data points within the evaluation period; 1-second granularity adds no detection advantage when the evaluation window is 60+ seconds.

severity_rules.name: large_count_high_resolution_alarms
severity_rules.description: Large number of high-resolution alarms detected. At scale, resolution choice has significant cost impact. Review each alarm namespace and evaluation period to confirm which, if any, genuinely require sub-minute granularity.

severity_rules.name: scenario_only_terraform_attributes
severity_rules.condition: aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm uses resolution_seconds OR actual_required_resolution_seconds OR metric_type OR evaluation_period_minutes
severity_rules.description: Terraform contains scenario-only attributes not valid in the AWS provider. Replace with real provider attributes (period, evaluation_periods, comparison_operator, metric_name, statistic, threshold) in optimized output.

safe_to_downgrade_conditions: namespace is Custom/* with no documented real-time SLA
metric_count pattern shows no sub-minute volatility requiring immediate detection
do_not_downgrade_conditions: Explicit sub-minute SLA documented in tags or runbook
Namespace tied to payment, fraud, safety, or emergency response systems
Alarm feeds an anomaly detection model that requires 1-second granularity

terraform: Set period = 60 as the default in shared aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm Terraform modules.
Require a justified exception tag (e.g. high_resolution_reason) for any alarm with period < 60.
Block period = 1 in OPA/Sentinel/tfsec policy unless exception is approved.
aws: Enable AWS Config custom rule to flag aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm with period < 60 lacking an exception tag.
Use Cost Explorer with CloudWatch metric dimension to track per-alarm metric cost.
Add cost allocation tags (alarm_name, namespace, team) to surface resolution cost to owning teams.
reporting: Report high-resolution vs standard alarm count and total metric cost separately.
Include API call overhead savings when cost report provides PutMetricData cost.
Use region-specific pricing when available; label static prices as estimates.
